perturbation.original_text: The Parties acknowledge that Information and/or data disclosed under this Non- Disclosure Agreement may include Personal Information (as such term is defined in the ISA Exhibit attached hereto). To the extent Personal Information is disclosed under this Non- Disclosure Agreement, the Parties obligations shall be governed by the Information Security Addendum (attached hereto as the ISA Exhibit) which is hereby incorporated by reference and explicitly made a part of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties acknowledge that Information and/or data disclosed under this Non- Disclosure Agreement may include Personal Information. To the extent Personal Information is disclosed under this Non- Disclosure Agreement, the Parties obligations shall be governed by the Information Security Addendum (attached hereto as the ISA Exhibit) which is hereby incorporated by reference and explicitly made a part of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase (as such term is defined in the ISA Exhibit attached hereto), the meaning of 'Personal Information' becomes ambiguous, and there is no longer a clear definition within the context of this specific NDA. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because it implies that 'Personal Information' is governed by the Information Security Addendum. The ISA Exhibit provides a specific definition of Personal Information that is now missing, leading to confusion. This is further emphasized by the fact that the definition in the ISA exhibit defines Personal Information as defined under Applicable Law, creating a contradiction as to what laws govern this personal information being discussed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 “Personal Information” – means information defined as “personal information or “personal data” under applicable Law. Without limiting the foregoing, Personal Information includes information that identifies or could be used to re-identify a specific person, including but not limited to first name and last name or first initial and last name in combination with any one or more of the following data elements: addresses; residential and/or mobile telephone numbers; e-mail addresses; social security numbers; medical insurance numbers; state issued identification card number (including tribal identification numbers); driver’s license numbers or other driver identification data; personnel records; financial account information; credit related information, including any information relating to credit checks or background checks; credit or debit card numbers and personal identification numbers such as access codes, security codes or passwords that would permit access to an individual’s financial account; and medical or health information. Without limiting the foregoing, Personal Information includes all private data of National Grid and its affiliates’ employees, officers, directors, subcontractors, agents, and customers, that Contractor receives from National Grid, and as may be defined by applicable state and/or federal statutes and regulations. Personal Information shall not include publicly available information, lawfully made available to the general public in federal, state, or local government records.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines 'Personal Information' as defined in the ISA Exhibit. The changed text removes this specific definition, causing an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text provides a specific definition of 'Personal Information' including applicable law. The omission in the changed text creates ambiguity and uncertainty about what definition should be used, which directly conflicts with the clear definition provided in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction, as essential language was removed which contradicts the definition provided in the ISA exhibit, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall not be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems, or (B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall not be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'or (B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain', it creates a contradiction. The Recipient is now obligated to destroy all information regardless if other laws require it to retain the data. Then, the following sentence specifically references clause (B), which now doesnt exist
perturbation.contradicted_text: §7. Return or Destruction of Information . Recipient shall return and deliver, or cause to be returned and delivered, to Disclosing Party, or destroy or cause to be destroyed (with certification of destruction delivered to Disclosing Party), all tangible Information, including copies and abstracts thereof, within thirty (30) days of a written request by Disclosing Party (a “ Request ”). The foregoing notwithstanding, Recipient may retain one (1) copy of such Information for archival purposes only and subject to compliance with the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, each Party agrees that Recipient shall not be required to return to Disclosing Party, or destroy, copies of Disclosing Party’s Information that (A) reside on Recipient’s or its Representatives’ backup, disaster recovery or business continuity systems. Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause '(B) that Recipient or its Representatives are obligated by applicable law and/or governmental regulations to retain'. This creates a direct contradiction with the sentence 'Recipient agrees that, following its receipt of the Request, it shall neither retrieve nor use Disclosing Party’s Information for any purpose other than that specified in clause (B) above', because clause (B) no longer exists in the modified text. This means that the Recipient is now obligated to destroy all information regardless of other laws. The contradicted text is actually located in the same place, but the change is what causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information or CEII, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Personal Information' is omitted from the exception clause in the first sentence, defining the default term. The second sentence however includes 'Personal Information' when it defines the 'Special Information Term.' This creates an in-text contradiction. On one hand it implies it has no end date on confidentiality, where on the other it specifies a Special Information Term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: §9. Term and Termination . Except with respect to any Information that is Customer Information or CEII, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall have a term of [ ] [()] years from the Effective Date (the “ Term ”), but in no event will the confidentiality obligations herein terminate less than one (1) year from the date of the last disclosure. In the case of any Information that is Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, Recipient’s obligations and duties under this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall survive for (i) the Term, or (ii) so long as such Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information, as applicable, is required to be kept confidential under applicable law, whichever period is longer (the “ Special Information Term ”). Either Party may terminate this Non-Disclosure Agreement by written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding any such termination, all rights and obligations hereunder shall survive (i) for the Special Information Term for all Customer Information, CEII or Personal Information disclosed prior to such termination, and (ii) for the Term for all other Information disclosed prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'Personal Information' from the exception in the first sentence concerning the standard term, while the second sentence still includes 'Personal Information' when defining the 'Special Information Term'. This creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence, as modified, implies Personal Information has a default term, while the second implies it is subject to the 'Special Information Term'. The contradiction text is the same paragraph which has been changed. The original text included Personal Information in the exception, making the text consistent.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Contractor shall, from time to time during the term of the Non-Disclosure Agreement and for a period of seven (7) years thereafter, during regular business hours and upon reasonable notice, permit National Grid or its representatives to perform audits of Contractor’s facilities, equipment, books and records (electronic or otherwise), operational systems and such other audits as may be necessary to ensure: (a) Contractor’s compliance with this Addendum, (b) Contractor’s compliance with all applicable Law, and (c) Contractor’s financial and operational viability, including but not limited to Contractor’s internal controls, security policies, business resumption, continuity, recovery, and contingency plans.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Contractor shall, from time to time during the term of the Non-Disclosure Agreement and for a period of seven (7) years thereafter, during regular business hours and upon reasonable notice, permit National Grid or its representatives to perform audits of Contractor’s facilities, equipment, books and records (electronic or otherwise), operational systems and such other audits as may be necessary to ensure: (a) Contractor’s compliance with this Addendum, and (c) Contractor’s financial and operational viability, including but not limited to Contractor’s internal controls, security policies, business resumption, continuity, recovery, and contingency plans.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause (b) Contractor’s compliance with all applicable Law, National Grid is no longer entitled to audit the contractor for compliance with all applicable laws. However, the statement as a whole still implies compliance with applicable law. As several other sections require compliance with applicable law, this could be an in-text contradiction, making it unclear whether National Grid can actually audit for that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Contractor hereby agrees to comply with all Laws it receives from National Grid during the term of the Non-Disclosure Agreement and ensure that all subcontractors or vendors who have access to National Grid’s Personal Information comply with all Laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed National Grid to audit the contractor for compliance with all applicable laws. The changed text removes this right. However, section 2.1 states that the contractor agrees to comply with all laws. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether National Grid can audit the contractor for legal compliance. The changed text significantly limits National Grid's auditing capabilities relative to the contractor's compliance obligations, leading to a direct conflict and thus a strong in-text contradiction.
